4 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.1 (issue #6, MVCC content versioning). WAL-before-apply wiring for VersionWriter.CommitVersion (engine/mvcc/write.go): CommitVersion now WAL-logs the version-pointer CAS (via existing wal.RecordCatalogPut/AppendAndApply, no new WAL record type) strictly before it is applied, closing the gap flagged by 2a.1.2's verification. Breaking change: CommitVersion gains a *wal.Writer parameter. Verified: no deadlock across commitLocks/Catalog-stripe/WAL-Writer interactions, WAL-before-apply and both crash-injection scenarios independently tested. Non-blocking: commitLocks sync.Map has no eviction, mirrors pre-existing states map limitation. Parent task-2a.1 stays planned; subtask 2a.1.5 pending.
Last of task-2a.1's 5 subtasks (issue #6, MVCC content versioning). All 5 subtasks (2a.1.1-2a.1.5) now verified. task-2a.1 and issue #6 closable.

Supersedes prior planned-state entry at line 6. Issue #6 closable (local tracking only, no live GH sync).

Verified 2026-07-04 (082-verification): EpochManager refcounting primitive correct in isolation (no negative refcounts, genuine double-release error test, MinReferencedEpoch hand-traced correct). Deferred wiring into Snapshot/CommitVersion judged acceptable scoping (corroborated by pre-implementation planner plan.json limiting this subtask's modules to gc.go/gc_test.go); 2a.2.2 verification must confirm the wiring actually lands.

Fix cycle: initial verification (2026-07-05-001-verification) returned CHANGES_REQUESTED on a genuine TOCTOU race in NewSnapshot (epoch acquired after reading CurrentVersion, allowing premature reclamation of a version a live snapshot still referenced). Fixed in 2026-07-05-002-implementation-fix (commits acc7601/afb3fbc): reordered NewSnapshot to acquire the epoch first, added regression test TestNewSnapshotClosesEpochAcquireVersionReadRace. Re-verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS in 2026-07-05-003-verification: happens-before proof independently re-derived against actual mutexes, race-free, zero regressions, 20x -race clean. Non-blocking: fix trades a slightly wider conservative-retention window for correctness.

Fix cycle: 006-verification returned CHANGES_REQUESTED (doc comment overclaimed equivalence to 2a.2.2's deterministic TOCTOU regression test -- disproved by a 30-run revert-and-rerun experiment showing zero detections; and readers using a fixed round count finished in ~7% of testDuration instead of overlapping writers/compactor throughout). Fixed in 007-implementation-fix (commit a1f220d): corrected doc comment to state this test complements but does not substitute for the deterministic regression test, and changed readers to loop on the shared stop signal for the full test duration with an in-test overlap assertion. Re-verified PASS_WITH_COMMENTS in 008-verification: readers now overlap ~100% of test duration (vs ~7% before), assertion correctly wired, zero regressions, clean at 10x/20x -race. Test-only fix cycle; no production code touched. Last of task-2a.2's 3 subtasks; task-2a.2 and issue #7 now closable.

Supersedes earlier planned entry. All 3 subtasks now verified: 2a.2.1 (EpochManager primitive, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS), 2a.2.2 (live snapshot/commit wiring, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS after one fix cycle), 2a.2.3 (GC concurrency stress test, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS after one test-only fix cycle). Closes issue #7 (epoch-based garbage collection) in full.

Added TestStripedConcurrencyStress: 2000 fileIDs, one goroutine each, 5 fixed Put/Delete patterns keyed by fileID%5, oracle derived statically per pattern; -race clean, full package no regressions, stable across -count=3.

Added BenchmarkStripedVsGlobalLock (engine/catalog/catalog_bench_test.go): Striped/GlobalLock sub-benchmarks over a minimal in-memory harness (reuses real stripeFor(fileID) + CatalogRecord.Encode/Decode) differing only in lock granularity (256 stripes vs 1 global mutex); real disk-backed Catalog/FileManager wrapping was tried first but pread/pwrite syscall cost masked the lock-contention signal. Measured: Striped ~230 ns/op vs GlobalLock ~454 ns/op (~2x), consistent across repeated runs; independently re-confirmed 3x during verification. -race -v -count=1 clean, no regressions, build/vet/gofmt clean. Last subtask of task-2a.3 (issue #8); parent task and issue now closable.

Supersedes earlier planned entry. Both subtasks now verified: 2a.3.1 (concurrent stress test across fileIDs verifying stripe isolation, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) and 2a.3.2 (contention benchmark: striped mutex vs global-lock baseline, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS). Closes issue #8 (Catalog concurrent-correctness hardening) in full.

1 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.4 (issue #9, latch-crabbing B+Tree concurrency), the last task of Phase 2a. Added engine/btree/latch.go: NodeStore-owned map[uint64]*nodeLatch registry (plain sync.Mutex for writer-only crabbing, atomic.Uint64 version counter, single-increment-after-mutation scheme). WriteNode bumps version by exactly 1 per successful write but does not itself acquire the latch (documented convention: 2a.4.2/2a.4.3 crabbing call sites must Lock/Unlock around WriteNode). insert.go/delete.go call sites intentionally left unwired. Verification confirmed lazy-population race is NOT present, WriteNode is the sole mutation choke-point, and the 50-goroutine concurrent test is genuine. Two non-blocking findings (registry has no eviction; node.go's old doc comments are now stale) tracked in .cdr/memory/pending.md. Parent task-2a.4 stays planned; subtasks 2a.4.2-2a.4.5 pending.

Round 3 (fourth agent dispatch): orchestrator personally caught the round-3 stress test (160g/80k) genuinely DEADLOCKED for 40+ minutes (goroutine dump: scratchpad/goroutine_dump_deadlock.log) -- multiple goroutines permanently blocked on sync.Mutex.Lock inside NodeStore.Lock, called from Tree.findParent/Tree.propagate, each waiting on a latch held by another blocked goroutine: a genuine lock-ordering CYCLE, not just a data race, and very likely the same underlying defect behind 024-verification's unconfirmed silent-data-loss symptom. Root cause confirmed: crabInsert/findParent's hand-over-hand ('lock next, release current') descent used a blocking Lock for every step; because this tree's design lets a node be reached both via its parent's already-linked Children entry AND via a not-yet-linked sibling's NextLeaf/NextSibling pointer at the same time, two concurrent crabbing walks could end up each holding a latch the other needed. Structural fix: every hand-over-hand step in crabInsert/findParent now uses NodeStore.TryLock (new method, latch.go); on a miss, the walk releases everything it holds and restarts from the root (errRestartFromRoot, insert.go) after a small jittered backoff, making a lock-ordering cycle structurally impossible by construction (no goroutine can ever block on a latch while holding a different one). Added TestCrabbingConcurrentPropagateNoDeadlock (insert_test.go): a fast (<0.1s), deterministic regression test using a synchronization hook (crabRetryHook), in the same spirit as engine/mvcc's hook-based pause pattern, to force the exact TryLock-collision interleaving without relying on large-scale random contention. Re-ran the 160g/80k TestCrabbingInsert suite plus the new regression test at -count=10 -race -timeout 5m (165s, all pass, no hang) and the full engine module at -race -timeout 5m (all pass). Also confirmed (again) that round 2's sort.Search fix was a no-op equivalent to the pre-fix 'pos := j' formula; reverted to the simpler form. Cleaned up all round-3 temporary diagnostics (debugWriteHook, zzrepro_instrumented_test.go); kept the O(1) promoted-key bounds check in propagate as a permanent invariant. Superseded the abandoned 2026-07-05/025-implementation-fix run (cut off mid-experiment, never reached implementation) with 2026-07-06/001-implementation-fix. Does NOT independently re-confirm the original silent-data-loss symptom is fixed by this specific change (plausible given the shared mechanism, but not separately proven) -- flagging for /cdr:verify. VERIFIED (2026-07-06-002-verification, 4th pass): deadlock structurally eliminated (30 additional clean repro runs at 160g/80k), silent-data-loss symptom not reproduced in 30 own runs (favorable, not airtight). Subtask closed; GitHub issue #9 resolved. Two low-risk non-blocking items carried forward (no retry-attempt metric; data-loss absence not mathematically proven).

3 of 5 subtasks under task-2a.4 (issue #9, latch-crabbing B+Tree concurrency). Concurrent Tree.Delete via latch-crabbing, reusing 2a.4.2's TryLock + full-release + restart-from-root discipline unmodified. One fix round: 005-verification returned CHANGES_REQUESTED because findLeftNeighborAtSameLevel's dangling-NextSibling repair after an ancestor splice only handled the same-grandparent (gj > 0) case; whenever the spliced ancestor was its grandparent's first child (gj == 0), the true left neighbor under an adjacent grandparent was deterministically never patched, a standing corruption risk since NextSibling is load-bearing for move-right routing during live descent. 006-implementation-fix (f949d54) generalized the repair to a walk-up-then-descend-down algorithm correct at any nesting depth, and fixed the regression test to exercise the concurrent Tree.Delete path (it had previously called the single-threaded free-function Delete and never reached the code under test). 007-verification returned PASS, with one non-blocking test_coverage comment: the multi-level-nested gj==0 case was verified only via a temporary, non-committed test; a committed regression test for that case is recommended follow-up. See .cdr/commits/task-2a.4.3.md for full record.

Tree.Lookup: lock-free optimistic version-counter read, never calls Lock/TryLock; version-before/ReadNode/version-after per node incl. Blink-tree move-right siblings; mismatch restarts whole lookup from root (no cap, mirrors crabInsert/crabDelete). Torn-read residual risk (single 4096B ReadAt/WriteAt page-atomicity assumption, not a POSIX guarantee) documented as deferred, non-blocking. Awaiting /cdr:verify.
